title: Inconsistent Function EXP in TiDB and TiKV
description:
## Bug Report

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

```sql
create table t0 (c0 double);
insert into t0 values  (5.84);
select EXP(LN(c0)) <= c0 from t0 where EXP(LN(c0)) <= c0; -- {0}
```

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)
`EXP(LN(c0))` is evaluated to `5.84` in TiKV, but `5.840000000000001` in TiDB, resulting in an unexpected result.
`EXP(LN(c0)) <= c0` is evaluated as true in `where`, which is executed in TiKV, but `EXP(LN(c0)) <= c0` is evaluated as false in `select`.

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)

`0`

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)

```bash
+--------------------------------------------+
| version()                                  |
+--------------------------------------------+
| 8.0.11-TiDB-v9.0.0-beta.1.pre-563-gea52376 |
+--------------------------------------------+
```
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1 (c1 FLOAT NOT NULL, c2 FLOAT DEFAULT NULL);
INSERT INTO t1 (c1, c2) VALUES (3.14, 2.71), (5.84, NULL), (NULL, 1.41);
SELECT EXP(LN(c1)) AS exp_ln_c1, c1 FROM t1 WHERE c1 IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: Inconsistent evaluation of EXP and LN functions
